title: Houses for rent near Cleveland State University (CSU)
metaTitle: Houses for Rent Near Cleveland State University
metaDescription: Explore houses for rent near Cleveland State University (CSU) — downtown transit, rent ranges, and Section 8-friendly options. Book a showing today.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent near cleveland state university
answerBox: Cleveland State University sits in downtown Cleveland along the Euclid Avenue corridor, reachable by GCRTA's HealthLine bus rapid transit and the Tower City rail hub. Nearby rental houses track the citywide median of about $1,250 a month, and Rent Finder Cleveland manages Section 8-friendly homes across the wider east and southeast side.

sections.h2: Where is Cleveland State University located?
body: Cleveland State University sits inside downtown Cleveland's official Statistical Planning Area, along the Euclid Avenue corridor a short walk from Public Square and the Tower City rail hub. The **Central** neighborhood, one of the city's 34 planning areas and historically a public-housing district, borders campus just to the southeast.

Because CSU is downtown rather than in a residential neighborhood, renters looking for a full house near campus usually end up searching just outside the core Downtown SPA. **[Houses for rent near downtown Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-near-downtown-cleveland/)** covers the closest options in more detail, including the Central and St. Clair-Superior areas that flank the university on either side of Euclid Avenue.

sections.h2: Getting to and from CSU without a car
body: CSU sits directly on GCRTA's **HealthLine**, a bus rapid transit route running along Euclid Avenue between Public Square and University Circle, so a car isn't required to reach downtown, Midtown, or the University Circle institutions.

**Tower City**, a few blocks from campus, is where every GCRTA rail line converges: the heavy-rail **Red Line** runs one direction to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport and the other to Windermere station in East Cleveland, while the **Blue** and **Green** light-rail lines head to Shaker Heights and the **Waterfront Line** reaches the lakefront Flats. Red Line trains run roughly every 15 minutes throughout the day.

sections.h2: What do rental houses cost near downtown Cleveland?
body: Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent was **$1,250 a month** as of Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, up 4.2% year over year, with 2-bedroom units running around $1,100 and 3-bedroom units near $1,350. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report puts the average rent for professionally managed apartments higher, at **$1,564** for about 787 square feet — a figure that leans toward newer, larger buildings rather than the older housing stock typical of most Cleveland rental houses. Both numbers sit well under the roughly $1,950 national median Zumper reports.

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses near CSU?
body: Our current portfolio of 90+ rental homes is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side — neighborhoods like Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — with a smaller number of homes on the west side. We don't list houses inside the Downtown SPA itself, but Fairfax and Central, both adjacent to CSU along the Euclid Avenue corridor, fall within our regular service area.

If you're renting near CSU, **[book a showing](/p/book-showing)** and tell us your target streets, budget, and move-in date. We'll match you against current listings or flag you when something opens up nearby, rather than promise a specific unit up front.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. The **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)** administers the local voucher program, but **Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law**, and the City of Cleveland itself does not currently protect voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination — only a handful of suburbs, including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights, have passed local protections. Read more in our **[Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/)**.

faq.q: Are there houses for rent near Cleveland State University?
faq.a: CSU sits in downtown Cleveland, where dedicated rental houses are limited, so most renters look at the adjacent Central, Fairfax, and Hough areas instead. Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly rental homes in Fairfax and other nearby east-side neighborhoods — book a showing to see current options close to campus.

faq.q: How do I get to CSU without a car?
faq.a: CSU is on GCRTA's HealthLine bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue, connecting Public Square to University Circle. Tower City, a short walk from campus, is the hub where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines all meet, so most of the city is reachable without driving.

faq.q: What's the average rent near downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, Cleveland's citywide median asking rent is about $1,250 a month. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report puts the average for professionally managed apartments higher, at $1,564, because that figure weights newer, larger buildings more heavily than the city's older rental stock.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Not directly inside the Downtown Statistical Planning Area, but our portfolio includes homes in Fairfax and Central, both adjacent to CSU along the Euclid Avenue corridor, as part of a wider concentration of rental homes across Cleveland's east and southeast side.

faq.q: Do CSU-area rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Keep in mind the City of Cleveland does not have its own source-of-income protection ordinance, unlike a few nearby suburbs, so always confirm voucher acceptance directly with any other landlord.
